nodes.label: Mermaid/Ester Character Visual Style
nodes.rationale: All characters must look like they belong in the same game: clean 2D sprites, large rounded shapes, solid/flat colors, highly readable silhouettes, simple gentle faces, minimal small details, transparent background. Must work at small in-game scale. Avoids dark outlines, realistic rendering, pixel art, texture painting, complex shadows, bright highlights.

nodes.label: Siren Reference Sprites
nodes.rationale: Four reference sprites at docs/storyboard/ref-teacher/ (baby-siren.png, child-siren.png, young-siren.png, adult-siren.png) define the visual baseline. Key traits: no dark outline, anatomy from simple masses (circles, ovals, drops, thick ribbons), minimal face, warm coral/peach colors.

nodes.label: Professor Octopus NPC Example
nodes.rationale: Example of translating concept art into the game's flat style. From octopus professor concepts → gentle elder with glasses, light hair, simple book, lavender shawl, large clean tentacles. Demonstrates reducing concepts to large readable shapes.

nodes.label: Character Chroma-Key Background Workflow
nodes.rationale: Generate with #00ff00 chroma-key background, ensure character uses no similar green, remove chroma-key locally, validate final PNG has real alpha, crop excess transparent canvas before placing in Assets.xcassets.
